title: Versioned SQL Migrations, Backfills, Compatibility, and Recovery · Type Forensics
summary: A preserved TypeScript feed-service trace contains one plausible discovery, URL, XML, validator, Fetch, stream, SQL, transaction, Promise, lease, Express, accessibility, migration, or release defect; find the earliest invalid transition before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in compatible TypeScript service migration chain and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causa…
objectives: Keep immutable ordered migrations, checksums, ownership, lock behavior, status inspection, and generated artifact review under source control.
Choose transactional and nontransactional boundaries for locks, concurrent indexes, data changes, and failure recovery.
Stage additive schema, bounded backfill, compatibility reads and writes, validation, cutover, and cleanup across deployed versions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Feed Ts Migration Ledger
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug TypeScript team handles case 7257 during a clean emitted-package and restore smoke. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove immutable migrations, expand-contract compatibility, resumable backfill, and recovery; every external value must remain unknown until current runtime validation admits it; then change one emitted artifact, package, container, incident, backup, restore, rollback, or capacity condition and reconcile evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Keep immutable ordered migrations, checksums, ownership, lock behavior, status inspection, and generated artifact review under source control.
